summary: Japan’s Corporate Governance Code, first introduced in 2015, has driven significant changes across the market – strengthening shareholder value, enhancing investor engagement, protecting minority rights, promoting gender diversity, and supporting the transition to a decarbonized economy. A further revision expected in 2026 could accelerate progress across these areas.

title: Odd Lots: Martin Wolf on the Roots of Our Recent Chaos (Podcast)
title_cn: 奇怪的事情：马丁·沃尔夫谈我们最近混乱的根源（播客）
summary: Last year, when we talked to Martin Wolf, the global order seemed like it was being upended after President Trump unveiled his sweeping tariffs against nearly every US trading partner. A lot has happened since then. In fact, April 2025 seems almost quaint when compared to 2026 so far, from the Supreme Court’s tariff ruling to the US-Israel war with Iran. The war’s effect on the world’s economy is at once stunning and utterly strange: even as the prices of major commodities — oil chief among them

title: Wyne: Trump has Little Interest in Taiwan Security

summary:
Presidents Donald Trump and Xi Jinping signalled a relationship reset as the two leaders set a positive tone at their historic summit in Beijing. Meanwhile President Xi warned that Taiwan remains the most critical issue in China-US relations. Ali Wyne, Senior Research and Advocacy Advisor for U.S.-China Relations at the International Crisis Group spoke to Bloomberg’s Horizons Middle East and Africa anchor Joumanna Bercetche on the visit between the two leaders.

 (Source: Bloomberg)

summary: Elon Musk’s xAI has recruited multiple Wall Street firms with ties to the billionaire’s business empire to test its Grok chatbot, according to people familiar with the matter, part of a push to bolster revenue ahead of parent company SpaceX’s initial public offering.

title: Hackers Are Already Using AI to Beef Up Their Attacks, Hide Their Activity
title_cn: 黑客已经在使用人工智能来加强他们的攻击，隐藏他们的活动
summary: Security personnel are catching attackers in new ways, too.

title: Anthropic in Talks to Raise $30 Billion at a $900 Billion Valuation
title_cn: Anthropic 正在洽谈融资 300 亿美元，估值达 9000 亿美元
summary: Anthropic PBC is in early talks with investors to raise at least $30 billion in fresh financing, according to people familiar with the matter, setting the stage for what could be its largest funding round yet. The Claude maker would then be valued at more than $900 billion. Bloomberg's Ed Ludlow reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: AI In Focus As Top CEOs Head To China For Trade Summit
title_cn: 顶级首席执行官前往中国参加贸易峰会，人工智能成为焦点
summary: US business leaders including Apple Inc.’s Tim Cook and Tesla Inc.’s Elon Musk on Trump’s first overseas trip since waging war in the Middle East — a 36-hour visit with Xi Jinping that’s expected to encompass the war, tariffs and the self-ruled island of Taiwan. The list of attendees until Tuesday had not included Huang, whose company makes the chips at the heart of the AI boom and has been pushing for greater leeway in a market he’s identified as a $50 billion opportunity. Bloomberg's Ed Ludlow joins Bloomberg Businessweek Daily to discuss.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Starmer Rivals Prepare to Challenge Weakened Prime Minister
title_cn: 斯塔默的竞争对手准备挑战被削弱的总理
summary: Keir Starmer’s efforts to hold back a potential leadership challenge showed new cracks, as allies of leading Labour Party rivals signaled they were ready to challenge him for the prime minister’s job.

title: Latvian Premier Resigns After Junior Party Withdraws Support
title_cn: 拉脱维亚总理在小党撤回支持后辞职
summary: Latvian Prime Minister Evika Silina announced her resignation on Thursday after a junior partner said it would no longer vote alongside her coalition, depriving the government of a majority in parliament.

title: UK Posts Strongest Growth in a Year Even as Headwinds Build
title_cn: 尽管逆风加剧，英国仍实现一年来最强劲增长
summary: Panmure Liberum economist Simon French discusses the UK economy and the political crisis surrounding Prime Minister Keir Starmer. Gross domestic product grew 0.6% in the first quarter, a jump from the 0.2% in the previous three months and the fastest expansion for a year. French speaks on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Ship Taken in Gulf of Oman and Heading to Iran, UK Navy Says
title_cn: 英国海军称，一艘在阿曼湾被扣押并前往伊朗的船只
summary: A UK naval group reported a commercial vessel off Fujairah in the United Arab Emirates was taken by unauthorized personnel and is now bound for Iranian waters.

title: Goldman Now Sees Two 2026 South Africa Rate Hikes Because of War
title_cn: 高盛现在预计南非 2026 年将因战争两次加息
summary: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. expects South Africa to raise interest rates twice this year after previously seeing a series of cuts, as the Iran war fans inflation risks around the world.

title: US Army Recovers Body of Second Soldier Missing in Morocco
title_cn: 美国陆军找到在摩洛哥失踪的第二名士兵的尸体
summary: The body of the second of two US soldiers who went missing during a military exercise in Morocco has been recovered and identified, the US Army said.

title: Xi Warns Trump of Possible ‘Clashes’ If Taiwan Issue Mishandled
title_cn: 习近平警告特朗普如果台湾问题处理不当可能发生“冲突”
summary: Xi Jinping warned Donald Trump of a potential conflict if the Taiwan issue is mismanaged, in blunt remarks that punctured an otherwise cordial start to the first trip to China by a sitting US president in nearly a decade.

title: Ex-Zelenskiy Aide Stole to Fund Luxury Home, Prosecutors Say
title_cn: 检察官称，前泽连斯基助手偷窃以资助豪宅
summary: Volodymyr Zelenskiy’s former chief of staff laundered stolen state funds through the construction of luxury real estate, according to Ukrainian prosecutors, even as he worked with the president to fight off Russia’s full-scale invasion.

title: Xi Tells US CEOs Accompanying Trump That China Will Open Up More
title_cn: 习近平向陪同特朗普的美国CEO表示中国将进一步开放
summary: Xi Jinping signaled China is moving toward greater openness, striking an upbeat note during his meeting with US business leaders accompanying Donald Trump as the two presidents wrapped up morning talks that featured more divisive issues like trade and Taiwan.

title: Myanmar Junta’s Forced Remittance Rules Pull in $5.6 Billion
title_cn: 缅甸军政府强制汇款规则吸引56亿美元资金
summary: Myanmar has turned migrant remittances into its biggest source of foreign inflows, finance ministry data seen by Bloomberg News show, after the military imposed rules that pressure citizens abroad to send money home.

title: What to Expect From the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing
title_cn: 对北京特习会有何期待
summary: President Donald Trump became the first US leader in almost a decade to visit China when he landed in Beijing on Wednesday. His much-anticipated two-day summit with President Xi Jinping — delayed from March due to the Iran war — offers the leaders of the world’s two largest economies a chance to reset personal ties and look for common ground on trade, technology and a range of other contentious topics.

title: India Faces Challenge of Bridging BRICS Rift Over Iran War
title_cn: 印度面临弥合金砖国家在伊朗战争问题上分歧的挑战
summary: India faces the challenge of bridging deep divisions within the BRICS bloc gathering in New Delhi this week, as members remain split over the Middle East conflict.

title: Trump-Xi Meeting Focuses on Taiwan Issue
title_cn: 习特会聚焦台湾问题
summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping pressed US President Donald Trump on the issue of Taiwan and warned of a potential conflict if mismanaged. The two leaders met Thursday morning at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing following an elaborate welcome ceremony during the first visit to China by a sitting US president in nearly a decade. Bloomberg's Minmin Low reports from Beijing. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Rayner Prepares Bid for Starmer’s Job After Tax Probe Ends
title_cn: 税务调查结束后，雷纳准备竞购斯塔默的工作
summary: Angela Rayner, the UK’s former deputy prime minister, has been cleared of deliberate wrongdoing by an investigation into her tax affairs and is preparing for a potential leadership bid against her former boss, Keir Starmer.

title: Poll Shows UK Public Divided Over When Starmer Should Resign
title_cn: 民意调查显示英国公众对于斯塔默何时应该辞职存在分歧
summary:
New polling by Ipsos shows that two in three Britons believe UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer should not lead the Labour Party into the next general election. But they are divided on whether he should resign immediately or sometime between now and the next election. Keiran Pedley, Director of UK Politics at Ipsos joined Lizzy Burden on Bloomberg Daybreak Europe. He said the public are the most pessimistic they have been about the economy for 48 years, and that voters are looking for an "answer" to the cost of living crisis, an issue that Labour "has to grapple with". 
Labour is headed for a divisive leadership battle. Former Deputy Prime Minister Angela Rayner has been cleared of deliberate wrongdoing by an investigation into her tax affairs and is preparing for a potential leadership bid while allies of Health Secretary Wes Streeting said he was likely to quit his post this week and launch a bid to replace Starmer as Labour leader.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Reeves Warns of ‘Chaos’ Stemming From UK Political Turmoil
title_cn: 里夫斯警告英国政治动荡引发“混乱”
summary: Keir Starmer Photographer: Jack Taylor/WPA Pool/Getty Images

title: Xi Says Talks With Trump Lay Out Vision for Stable Ties With US
title_cn: 习近平表示与特朗普的会谈制定了稳定中美关系的愿景
summary: Donald Trump and Xi Jinping at the Temple of Heaven.

title: Opposition Seeks US Sanctions for Uganda’s Seventh-Term Leader
title_cn: 反对派寻求美国制裁乌干达第七任领导人
summary: Self-exiled Ugandan opposition leader Robert Kyagulanyi is making the rounds in Washington urging US senators to press for targeted sanctions against his country’s octogenarian leader, who’s just been sworn in for a seventh term in office.

title: Chinese Public Greets Trump in Beijing With AI Memes, Confidence
title_cn: 中国公众在北京用人工智能表情包和信心迎接特朗普
summary: People in China were having fun watching the landmark visit by US President Donald Trump, creating AI memes and telling light-hearted jokes about his high-profile entourage, though some also complained about disruptions the visit is causing.

title: Lithuania Says US Paused Rotation of Troops to Europe
title_cn: 立陶宛称美国暂停向欧洲轮调部队
summary: Lithuanian Defense Minister Robertas Kaunas said the US has paused rotation of troops to Europe as the Pentagon reviews its deployment plans on the continent.

title: India Asks US for Russian Waiver Extension as Iran War Drags On
title_cn: 随着伊朗战争的继续，印度要求美国延长俄罗斯的豁免
summary: India has asked the US to extend its waiver on Russian oil, according to people familiar with the matter, as the nearly 11-week war in the Persian Gulf disrupts energy supply.

title: Philippines' Political Tension Escalates After Senate Gunfire
title_cn: 参议院枪击事件后菲律宾政治紧张局势升级
summary: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. has called for calm after gunshots were fired on Wednesday evening at the Philippine Senate, where a lawmaker wanted by the International Criminal Court has sought refuge. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Duterte Ally Sought Over Drug War Escapes After Senate Gunfire
title_cn: 杜特尔特盟友在参议院枪击事件后追捕毒品战争逃亡者
summary: The lawmaker at the center of escalating political chaos in the Philippines has left the Senate chamber’s premises where he was holed up this week, hours after gunfire rang out at the complex, according to a top official.

title: Can Thailand’s Powerful Shinawatra Family Make a Political Comeback?
title_cn: 泰国实力派西那瓦家族能否在政坛东山再起？
summary: Former Thai prime minister Thaksin Shinawatra — one of Thailand’s most polarizing but enduring political figures since he became prime minister in 2001 — was jailed in September for corruption after returning to the country from years of self-imposed exile. Just eight months later, on May 11, he walked out of a Bangkok prison having served parts of a sentence reduced by royal pardon.

title: CFR’s Liu, CSIS’s Levin on Xi-Trump Summit Stakes
title_cn: CFR 的 Liu 和 CSIS 的 Levin 谈习近平与特朗普峰会的利害关系
summary: Council on Foreign Relations Senior Fellow Zongyuan Zoe Liu and Center for Strategic and International Studies Senior Fellow Henrietta Levin unpack the significance of the summit between Chinese President Xi Jinping and US President Donald Trump. Liu says the summit itself is already the deliverable, while Levin notes China is entering the talks from a position of strength. They speak with Haslinda Amin on "Insight with Haslinda Amin." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Europe Plays Catch-Up in Africa as Emerging Powers Boost Investment
title_cn: 新兴国家加大投资，欧洲在非洲迎头赶上
summary: A summit in Nairobi yielded €14 billion in French investment commitments, but Europeans have fallen far behind newer arrivals in the region.

title: Bolsonaro Ties to Banco Master CEO Rock Brazil Presidential Race
title_cn: 博尔索纳罗与 Banco Master 首席执行官携手推动巴西总统竞选
summary: Leaked audio messages linking Brazilian presidential candidate Flavio Bolsonaro to the man at the center of a multibillion-dollar bank fraud scandal are threatening to torpedo the right-wing senator’s campaign before it even gets started.
